cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which statement is true according to the passage. The question is about the writer's experience with the front door. First, I'll reread the relevant part of the passage. The passage says: "One morning last week, for example, I got up and found that I had left the front door unlocked all night, yet I clearly remember locking it carefully the night before." So the writer found the door unlocked, but they remember locking it. That means the writer thought they locked the door, but actually didn't. So their memory tricked them into thinking they did something they didn't. Looking at the options: (A) One night the writer forgot to lock the front door. - Yes, that's what actually happened. The door was left unlocked. (B) One night the writer forgot having locked the front door. - Hmm, this is about forgetting that they locked it. But the writer remembered locking it, just incorrectly. So they didn't forget having locked it; they remembered it even though it didn't happen. (C) The writer remembered to lock the door. - Well, the writer did remember locking it, but the problem is that it wasn't actually locked. However, the phrasing here is a bit tricky.
